ReqMaster.system_instructions:
You are a Requisition Master agent handling job intake and requirements analysis.

Your responsibilities:
1. Retrieve and validate job posting details
2. Extract structured requirements (must-have vs nice-to-have)
3. Verify job is approved and open for sourcing
4. Identify hiring manager and team context
5. Set up screening criteria for candidate matching

When processing job requisitions:
- Validate the job ID exists and is in 'open' status
- Extract all required and preferred skills
- Note salary range and location requirements
- Identify urgency level and time-to-fill targets
- Flag any special requirements (clearance, certifications)

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- If the position is time-sensitive or high priority, include the word "urgent" in your analysis
- If the role requires international hiring or global compliance, include "international" in your response
- Always clearly state the priority level (high/medium/low)

Output format: Provide structured job requirements ready for candidate sourcing.

tool_definitions.name: get_job_details
tool_definitions.description: Retrieves detailed information about a job posting including title, department, requirements, salary range, and hiring manager.

job_id.description: The job ID to retrieve (e.g., JOB-SWE-2025-001)
job_id.title: Job Id

tool_definitions.name: extract_requirements
tool_definitions.description: Extracts and structures the requirements from a job posting, separating must-have from nice-to-have skills.

job_id.description: The job ID to extract requirements from
job_id.title: Job Id

CommsBot.system_instructions:
You are a Communications Bot handling candidate correspondence.

Your responsibilities:
1. Send interview invitation emails
2. Provide application status updates
3. Send rejection notifications professionally
4. Coordinate offer letter delivery
5. Handle candidate inquiries

When communicating with candidates:
- Use professional, warm tone
- Include all relevant details (time, location, etc.)
- Provide clear next steps
- Respect candidate's time and privacy
- Follow up on unanswered communications

Output format: Confirm email sent with recipient and template used.

tool_definitions.name: send_candidate_email
tool_definitions.description: Sends an email to a candidate. Use for interview invitations, rejections, or follow-ups.

parts.name: send_candidate_email
parts.arguments:
{"candidate_id":"CAND-EXT-001","template":"interview_invite","custom_message":"Dear Candidate,

We are pleased to invite you for an interview for the Senior Software Engineer position at our company. 

**Interview Details:**  
**Date:** [Insert Date]  
**Time:** [Insert Time]  
**Location:** [Insert Location/Link to Virtual Meeting]  

Please let us know your availability for this time or if you would prefer to propose another time. We are looking forward to speaking with you!

Best regards,  
[Your Company Name]  
[Your Contact Information]"}

parts.content: It seems that I couldn't find the candidate with ID "CAND-EXT-001." Please check the candidate ID and provide me with the correct one, or let me know if you would like to proceed with a different candidate.

Good direction. Now proceed with the communication plan.

Here's a structured communication plan to follow for the interview invitation process:

### Communication Plan for Interview Invitations

**1. Identify Candidates:**
   - Review the candidate database for valid IDs or names for the Senior Software Engineer position.

**2. Draft Invitation Template:**
   - Ensure the following template is ready to be sent to each identified candidate:
   
   ---
   **Subject:** Interview Invitation for Senior Software Engineer Position

   **Email Body:**

   Dear [Candidate Name],

   We are excited to invite you for an interview for the Senior Software Engineer position at [Your Company Name].

   **Interview Details:**  
   - **Date:** [Insert Date]  
   - **Time:** [Insert Time]  
   - **Location:** [Insert Zoom link or physical location]

   Please confirm your availability for this time or let us know if another time works better for you. We look forward to our conversation!

   Best regards,  
   [Your Name]  
   [Your Company Name]  
   [Your Contact Information]  
   ---

**3. Confirm and Send:**
   - Once candidates are identified, replace placeholders with specific details (name, date, time, location) and send the invitation emails.

**4. Follow-Up:**
   - If candidates do not respond within three days, send a follow-up email.
   
   ---
   **Subject:** Follow-Up on Interview Invitation

   **Email Body:**

   Dear [Candidate Name],

   I hope this message finds you well. I wanted to follow up regarding the interview invitation for the Senior Software Engineer position. If you have any questions or if another time would work better for you, please let me know.

   Looking forward to your response!

   Best regards,  
   [Your Name]  
   [Your Company Name]  
   [Your Contact Information]  
   ---

**5. Document and Record:**
   - Keep a record of sent invitations and responses to track the status of each candidate.

### Next Steps
- Review candidate records for valid IDs.
- Use the invitation template for each candidate.
- Set reminders for any necessary follow-ups.

Please let me know if you'd like me to clarify any part of this plan or assist further!

It seems I'm still unable to send emails due to ongoing issues with the candidate ID. 

### Alternative Next Steps

1. **Creation of a Draft Document:**
   - Prepare a document or template ready for use when valid candidate IDs are confirmed. This can be easily modified and sent later.
   
2. **Communicate with the HR Team:**
   - If applicable, draft a message to the HR team to verify the candidate IDs and gather the correct information needed to proceed.

3. **Prepare Follow-Up Email:**
   - Keep the follow-up email template ready to be used once invitations are sent.

### Draft Outreach to HR Team

---
**Subject:** Request for Candidate IDs for Interview Invitations

**Email Body:**

Dear [HR Team/Specific Person's Name],

I hope this message finds you well. I am in the process of sending out interview invitations for the Senior Software Engineer position and noticed some issues with candidate IDs. Could you please provide me with the correct IDs for the candidates so we can proceed with the invitations?

Your assistance is greatly appreciated!

Best regards,  
[Your Name]  
[Your Company Name]  
[Your Contact Information]  
---

If confirmed, I can tailor communications specifically once the valid candidate IDs are available. Please let me know if you would like to change focus or take a different approach!

Continuing autonomously, I'll implement the following steps to ensure progress in the recruitment process for the Senior Software Engineer position:

### Implementation of Immediate Actions

1. **Set Reminder for HR Follow-Up:**
   - Schedule a reminder to send the follow-up email to the HR team in 3 days.

2. **Prepare Documentation for Interviews:**
   - Organize templates and notes for interviewers, explaining the structure and focus areas for candidate evaluation.

3. **Create a Detailed FAQ Document:**
   - Develop a comprehensive FAQ document for candidates, addressing common queries such as:

   - **Role Responsibilities**
   - **Company Culture**
   - **Interview Process**

4. **Draft a Communication Plan for Candidates Post-Interview:**
   - Prepare a communication strategy outlining how to inform candidates about interview outcomes.

### Sample Communication for Post-Interview Updates

**Rejection Email:**
---
**Subject:** Update on Your Application for the Senior Software Engineer Position

**Email Body:**

Dear [Candidate Name],

Thank you for taking the time to interview for the Senior Software Engineer position at [Your Company Name]. We appreciate your effort and interest in our team.

After careful consideration, we regret to inform you that we will not be moving forward with your application at this time. We encourage you to apply for future positions as they become available.

Thank you again for your interest, and we wish you the best in your job search.

Best regards,  
[Your Name]  
[Your Company Name]  
[Your Contact Information]  
---

**Acceptance Email:**
---
**Subject:** Job Offer for Senior Software Engineer Position

**Email Body:**

Dear [Candidate Name],

We are pleased to inform you that you have been selected for the Senior Software Engineer position at [Your Company Name]. We believe that your skills and experiences will be a great addition to our team.

Details regarding your offer and next steps will be shared shortly.

Congratulations, and we look forward to working with you!

Best regards,  
[Your Name]  
[Your Company Name]  
[Your Contact Information]  
---

### Immediate Next Steps

1. **Finalize and store the aforementioned documents.**
2. **Prepare for potential candidate queries based on the established FAQs.**

I’ll continue to monitor the situation and maintain readiness for when the HR team responds or when candidate interactions arise. If you need any adjustments or additional actions, just let me know!
